raw_response:
I agree with the agent's assessment. The Km indeed signifies the substrate concentration at which the enzymatic reaction achieves half-maximal velocity (\(V_{max}/2\)). This value provides crucial information about the enzyme’s binding affinity for its substrate; lower Km values indicate higher affinity since more enzymes can bind substrates even at low concentrations.
